What is X26

The primary community for ex-McKinsey founders, investors & operators

X26 is the definitive home for ex-McKinsey founders, investors, and operators.

We support ex-McKinsey founders from Day 0 — before the raise, before the launch — and stay with them through their second and third ventures. Our community of 2,500+ spans the full arc of the startup journey, from first-time founders to seasoned operators and investors.

Every two weeks, we're somewhere in the world. Between events, we support our members through fundraising, YC applications, founder retreats, co-founder matching, and partnership events with the likes of MIT and Google.

01

Weekly Events

Regular programming, workshops, and founder sessions to keep you connected and sharp.

02

YC Support

Application support, mock interviews, and direct introductions to YC partners and alumni.

03

Co-founder Matching

Structured matching between complementary founders at every stage of the journey.

04

Founder Retreats

Immersive off-site retreats for deep work, peer learning, and lasting connections.

05

Mentorship

1:1 mentorship from operators, founders, and investors who've been where you're going.

06

Online Community

An active digital home with curated channels, deal flow, and peer support around the clock.

For McKinsey Founders, Built by McKinsey Founders
Leadership

The team behind X26

Ed Bramwell

Ed co-founded X26 in early 2022 and has driven its investment infrastructure. A former McKinsey consultant in London, he participated in Entrepreneur First before founding a FinTech, and recently founded his second startup at the intersection of AI and Financial Services.

Reece Griffiths

Reece co-founded X26 and went on to found Deasy Labs (YC S23), backed by Bessemer & General Catalyst, acquired by Collibra for a significant eight-figure sum in under two years. He spent 3 years at McKinsey and QuantumBlack before founding Deasy.

Gagan Khurana

Gagan co-founded X26 after working closely with startups through McKinsey's Fuel practice, and most recently scaled revenue at a Series A company from ~$100k to >$2m in 9 months. At X26, he makes introductions between community members to create meaningful connections.

Alexander Volz

Alexander joined as Founding Partner after operating as a McKinsey consultant, SaaS founder, and C-level leader at a unicorn. In 2025 alone, founders he coached added more than $1 billion in valuation. He co-leads founder retreats with Sam Bourton, CTO of QuantumBlack.

Melissa Griffin

Melissa leads community operations at X26, keeping our 2,500+ member network connected, engaged, and growing. She manages weekly programming, onboarding, and the partnerships that bring new opportunities to X26 founders globally.
